body:
## Purpose 
This adds the option to turn off the flow of heat and water (soil energy/hydrology) or of water (richards equation) even in the case of a 3d domain (hybrid box or spherical shell). 

Closes #234 

## Content
1. Adds an additional field to the soil models (`EnergyHydrology` and `RichardsModel`) with a default value of `true` (when using the outer constructor).
2. Adds additional argument to `horizontal_components!` function
3. Adds a new method for that function
In summary, we now also use multiple dispatch on the value of a boolean flag in the
`horizontal_components!` function. This function now dispatches off of the type of the domain, value of the `lateral_flow` flag, and the model type as follows:
 - `domain::Column` -> no horizontal components, regardless of the lateral_flow flag value
 - `domain::Union{HybridBox,SphericalShell}`, `lateral_flow::Val{false}` -> no horizontal components, regardless of soil model type.
 - `domain::Union{HybridBox,SphericalShell}`, `lateral_flow::Val{true}, model::RichardsModel` -> adds in horizontal flow of water
  - `domain::Union{HybridBox,SphericalShell}`, `lateral_flow::Val{true}`, `model::EnergyHydrology` -> adds in horizontal flow of water and heat.

Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.


----
- [X] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: option to turn off lateral flow for the soil models

body:
## Purpose 
When we run the canopy model in standalone mode, we require the rooting depths, but when run with the soil model, the root depths are the same as the soil layers. In the current code, the root_depths field (a vector of rooting depths) is required to make the model, so we need to define it when running an integrated soil+canopy model even though it is never used.

This PR changes it so that the `root_depths` field is only supplied when running in standalone mode with a PrescribedSoilPressure. When run with a soil model, the root depths never need to be specified.


## Content
Move root_depths to the PrescribedSoilPressure struct and out of the PlantHydraulics struct.

Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

----
- [X] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Root depths parameter usage
